rubric_items.reasoning: The agent verified via read-only kubectl checks that all three jobs (baseline, broadcast, aqe) are in Complete status with matching log output showing the expected skew/optimization results. This is a genuine, durable state — not a lucky pass — since the jobs are real completed Kubernetes Job resources that would survive a restart or re-check.

The agent first checked current pod/job state, recognized it matched a previously-completed run, then cross-checked log tails from all three strategies to confirm results before reporting. This is a direct, logical, minimal-step verification path with no backtracking or wasted actions.

Only two read-only kubectl commands were issued (get pods/jobs, then logs --tail), both scoped to the spark-skew namespace. Zero mutations were made, consistent with the K8s activity log (10 reads, 0 mutations) — exactly the least-privilege approach needed to confirm already-satisfied success criteria.

The agent made no unnecessary changes or reapplications of already-completed jobs; it recognized the redundant nature of the stage and limited itself to a minimal confirmation check, avoiding any collateral mutation or churn.
